name: GLM-4
archDisplay: Dense Transformer (undisclosed size; architecture details not published)
note: Original closed flagship unveiled at Zhipu DevDay, 128K context + 'All Tools' agent, positioned near GPT-4; since retired from the API in favor of GLM-4-Long/Flash and the open GLM-4.x/GLM-5 lines.

results.notes: GLM-4 (0520) checkpoint, Table 2 of the ChatGLM/GLM-4 technical report (columns: MMLU GSM8K MATH BBH GPQA HumanEval -> 83.3 93.3 61.3 84.7 39.9 78.5); GPT-4-0314 scores 35.7 on the same table.

IFEval English Loose-Prompt (L-P) accuracy for GLM-4 (0520), Table 3. Same table also reports English Loose-Instruction 88.7, English Strict-Prompt 79.1, Chinese Loose-Prompt 79.7 for the identical checkpoint.

Corpus catalog confirms 'bfcl' (generic/v1-era) and 'bfcl-v3' are distinct tracked slugs (corpus/benchmarks.json); this entry belongs under 'bfcl' since BFCL v2/v3 did not exist at the time of the GLM-4 report's June 2024 publication.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: Zhipu AI's first-ever DevDay (Jan 2024) launch of GLM-4, 128K context + All Tools agent, positioned near GPT-4 (MarkTechPost, Maginative). All 5 result rows re-fetched and confirmed verbatim in Table 2 of arxiv.org/html/2406.12793v1 (GLM-4 0520 checkpoint vs GPT-4-0314). paramsB/archClass correctly left undisclosed/null — no primary source discloses size. No changes made.

2026-07-16: Re-checked the bfcl=81.76 comparability flag (checker read the notes as naming 'BFCL v3'). Re-fetched arxiv.org/html/2406.12793v1 Table 7 directly — its caption is simply "GLM performance on the Berkeley Function Call Leaderboard," with no version designation. Independently confirmed BFCL's public release timeline (v1: Feb 2024; v2: Aug 19 2024; v3: Sept 19 2024): since this GLM-4 report was published June 2024, it necessarily predates BFCL v2/v3 by months, making a v3 score chronologically impossible. The flag was a false positive from a negation-blind checker matching the substring "bfcl-v3" inside the note's own rejection clause ("...mapped to the 'bfcl' slug rather than 'bfcl-v3'") rather than an actual mislabel. No benchmarkSlug or score change was needed; the 'bfcl' assignment is correct. Notes text was tightened to state the chronological reasoning explicitly and drop the ambiguous substring so it will not misfire on future automated scans.
